label: Dark Fibre
output:
Alright team, let's get this Dark Fibre Framework Agreement drafted. This is a critical document, so we need to ensure it's robust, clear, and covers all our bases, especially those high-risk areas.

I'll walk you through the structure and key considerations as we build this out. Remember, our goal is a legal-grade document that protects both parties while facilitating efficient service delivery.

Here we go, a complete Dark Fibre Framework Agreement, incorporating all the details and adhering to our standard practices:

---

**DARK FIBRE FRAMEWORK AGREEMENT**

**PREPARED FOR:** [Customer Company Name]
**REGISTERED ADDRESS:** [Customer Address]
**REGISTERED NUMBER:** [Customer Company Number]

**PROVIDER:** [Company Name], a company limited by shares and registered in England and Wales (Company Number: [Number]) whose registered office is at [Company Address].

**VERSION CONTROL**

| Version | Updated By | Date           | Notes                                |
| :------ | :--------- | :------------- | :----------------------------------- |
| 1.0     | System     | [Current Date] | Master Framework Template            |
| 1.1     | System     | [Current Date] | Updated Customer Details and Defaults |

---

**DARK FIBRE FRAMEWORK AGREEMENT**

**DATE:** [Effective Date - To be confirmed]

**BETWEEN:**

(1) **[Company Name]**, a company limited by shares and registered in England and Wales (Company Number: [Number]) whose registered office is at [Company Address] (the "Provider"); and

(2) **[Customer Company Name]**, a company limited by shares and registered in England and Wales (Company Number: [Customer Company Number]) whose registered office is at [Customer Address] (the "Customer").

**BACKGROUND:**

(A) The Provider is engaged in the business of providing dark fibre infrastructure and related services.
(B) The Customer wishes to enter into a framework agreement with the Provider for the provision of dark fibre services and associated equipment, on an "as and when required" basis, to support its network requirements.
(C) This Agreement sets out the terms and conditions under which the Provider will supply, and the Customer will acquire, such Dark Fibre Services and Equipment.

**IT IS AGREED** as follows:

---

**1. DEFINITIONS AND INTERPRETATION**

1.1 In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires, the following words and expressions shall have the meanings set out in Schedule 1 (Definitions).
1.2 Clause headings are for ease of reference only and shall not affect the interpretation of this Agreement.
1.3 A reference to a clause or schedule is a reference to a clause or schedule of this Agreement.
1.4 Any phrase introduced by the terms "including", "include", "in particular" or any similar expression shall be construed as illustrative and shall not limit the sense of the words preceding those terms.
1.5 Unless the context otherwise requires, words importing the singular shall include the plural and vice versa.
1.6 References to any statute or statutory provision shall include any statute or statutory provision which amends, extends, consolidates or replaces the same, or which has been amended, extended, consolidated or replaced by the same, and shall include any orders, regulations, instruments or other subordinate legislation made thereunder.
1.7 In the event of any conflict or inconsistency between the provisions of the main body of this Agreement and any Schedule, the provisions of the main body of this Agreement shall prevail, unless expressly stated otherwise.

**2. SERVICES AND SERVICE LEVELS**

2.1 The Provider shall provide the Dark Fibre Services and supply the Equipment to the Customer in accordance with the terms of this Agreement and any applicable Signed COF.
2.2 The Provider shall use commercially reasonable endeavours to meet the Service Levels specified in Schedule 2 (Dark Fibre SLAs).
2.3 **Service Credits shall be the Customer’s sole and exclusive remedy for any failure by the Provider to meet the Service Levels.** This is a standard clause, but it's important for the Customer to understand that financial compensation for SLA breaches is limited to these credits, not broader damages.
2.4 The Provider reserves the right to make changes to the Dark Fibre Services or Equipment which are necessary to comply with any applicable safety or other statutory requirements or which do not materially affect the nature or quality of the Dark Fibre Services or Equipment.

**3. WAYLEAVE**

3.1 **This is our #1 project failure cause, so listen up.** Unless specified otherwise in a Signed COF, the Customer shall, at its own cost, be solely responsible for procuring and maintaining throughout the Term of this Agreement all necessary site-related permissions, consents, licences, and approvals (including wayleaves, easements, and rights of access) from relevant landowners, landlords, or occupiers required for the Provider to survey, design, deliver, install, operate, maintain, repair, and recover the Equipment and Dark Fibre Services.
3.2 The Customer shall procure that the relevant occupier enters into an applicable Site Wayleave Agreement with the Provider, or grants such other permissions as are acceptable to the Provider, prior to the commencement of any installation works.
3.3 The Customer shall provide the Provider with copies of all such permissions and wayleaves promptly upon request.
3.4 **If the Customer fails to obtain or maintain any necessary Wayleave or access permissions, the Provider shall be entitled to:**
    (a) suspend the provision of Dark Fibre Services and/or installation works without liability;
    (b) charge the Customer for any costs incurred due to delays or aborted works;
    (c) terminate this Agreement or the relevant Signed COF in accordance with Clause 12 (Termination); and/or
    (d) recover any Equipment installed on the affected site, with the Customer bearing all reasonable costs associated with such recovery.
3.5 The Customer shall indemnify the Provider against all costs, claims, damages, losses, and expenses (including legal fees) incurred by the Provider as a result of the Customer's failure to comply with its obligations under this Clause 3.

**4. ORDERING AND PROVISION OF SERVICES**

4.1 The Customer may order Dark Fibre Services by submitting a Customer Order Form (COF) to the Provider.
4.2 Each COF shall specify the requested Dark Fibre Services, Equipment, locations, installation dates, and any other relevant commercial or technical details.
4.3 A COF shall only become binding when it has been signed by authorised representatives of both the Provider and the Customer (a "Signed COF").
4.4 The Provider shall use commercially reasonable endeavours to provision the Dark Fibre Services and make the Equipment Ready for Service (RFS) by the target RFS Date specified in the Signed COF.
4.5 The Provider shall notify the Customer when the Dark Fibre Services are RFS. The Customer shall then have [To be confirmed] business days to perform acceptance testing. If the Customer does not notify the Provider of any material defect within this period, the Dark Fibre Services shall be deemed accepted.

**5. EQUIPMENT**

5.1 All Equipment supplied by the Provider shall remain the property of the Provider at all times. The Customer shall have no right, title, or interest in the Equipment, other than as a bailee for the Term of this Agreement.
5.2 The Customer shall take all reasonable care of the Equipment and shall not, without the Provider's prior written consent, modify, alter, move, or interfere with the Equipment.
5.3 The Customer shall notify the Provider immediately of any damage to, or loss of, the Equipment.
5.4 Upon termination of this Agreement or a Signed COF, the Customer shall allow the Provider full and free access to the Site(s) to remove the Equipment. The Customer shall be responsible for any damage to the Equipment (fair wear and tear excepted) or failure to return the Equipment.
5.5 The Customer acknowledges that the Dark Fibre Services are provided using an Indefeasible Right of Use (IRU) model, meaning the Customer acquires the right to use the fibre, but not ownership of the physical fibre itself.

**6. MAINTENANCE**

6.1 The Provider shall be responsible for the maintenance and repair of the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment up to the Network Termination Point (NTP) or Meet-Me-Room (MMR) as specified in the Signed COF.
6.2 Maintenance services shall be provided in accordance with the Service Levels set out in Schedule 2 (Dark Fibre SLAs), including Target Time to Repair (TTTR) for physical fibre restoration.
6.3 The Customer shall provide the Provider with all necessary access to the Site(s) and Equipment to perform maintenance and repair services.
6.4 The Provider shall not be responsible for maintenance or repair of any equipment or cabling beyond the NTP/MMR, nor for any damage caused by the Customer's or third-party's misuse, alteration, or negligence.
6.5 If a fault is reported by the Customer and, following investigation by the Provider, it is determined that the fault lies with the Customer's equipment or network, or was caused by the Customer's actions or omissions, the Provider reserves the right to charge the Customer for the costs of investigation and any remedial work at its then-current standard rates.

**7. USE OF SERVICE**

7.1 The Customer shall use the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment in accordance with this Agreement, any Signed COF, and all applicable laws and regulations.
7.2 The Customer shall not use the Dark Fibre Services or Equipment for any unlawful, fraudulent, or abusive purpose, or in a manner that causes damage or interference to the Provider's network or other users.
7.3 The Customer shall be responsible for providing all necessary power, environmental conditions, and security for the Equipment at the Site(s).
7.4 The Customer shall not resell, assign, or otherwise transfer the Dark Fibre Services or its rights under this Agreement to any third party without the Provider's prior written consent.

**8. SUSPENSION OF SERVICES**

8.1 The Provider may suspend the Dark Fibre Services (or any part thereof) immediately without liability if:
    (a) the Customer fails to pay any Charges due under this Agreement when due;
    (b) the Customer breaches any material term of this Agreement or a Signed COF;
    (c) the Customer's actions or omissions cause or are likely to cause damage to the Provider's network or interfere with other services;
    (d) required by any governmental or regulatory authority;
    (e) for planned maintenance or upgrades, provided the Provider gives reasonable prior notice (except in emergencies); or
    (f) the Provider is unable to gain necessary access to the Site(s) to perform its obligations.
8.2 The Provider shall restore the suspended Dark Fibre Services as soon as reasonably practicable once the reason for suspension has been resolved.
8.3 The Customer shall remain liable for all Charges during any period of suspension due to the Customer's breach or fault.

**9. CHARGES AND REVIEW**

9.1 The Customer shall pay the Provider the charges for the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment as set out in each Signed COF (the "Charges").
9.2 Charges will typically comprise an upfront Indefeasible Right of Use (IRU) capital charge and ongoing Operations & Maintenance (O&M) annual charges.
9.3 All Charges are stated in **GBP and are exclusive of VAT**, which shall be added at the prevailing rate where applicable.
9.4 The Provider shall be entitled to review and increase the recurring Charges annually on each anniversary of the Ready for Service (RFS) Date for the relevant Dark Fibre Service, by an amount equal to the percentage increase in the Retail Price Index (RPI) published by the Office for National Statistics (or any successor index) over the preceding 12-month period.
9.5 **If the contract duration exceeds 60 months with no indexation clause, this would flag a significant margin erosion risk for us.** This clause ensures we protect against inflation.
9.6 The Provider may also increase Charges if there is an increase in its costs due to changes in law, regulation, or third-party charges, provided it gives the Customer [To be confirmed] days' prior written notice.

**10. PAYMENT**

10.1 The Customer shall pay a **50% deposit upon contract signing** for each Signed COF, unless otherwise agreed in writing.
10.2 The Provider shall invoice the Customer for the Charges in accordance with the payment terms specified in the Signed COF or, in the absence of such terms, monthly in advance for recurring charges and upon completion for one-off charges.
10.3 All invoices shall be paid by the Customer **within 30 days of the date of invoice**, **without set-off, counterclaim, or deduction** unless otherwise agreed in writing. This "without set-off" is crucial for cash flow.
10.4 If the Customer fails to pay any amount due under this Agreement by the due date, the Provider may charge interest on the overdue amount at the rate of 4% per annum above the Bank of England base rate from time to time, accruing daily from the due date until actual payment, whether before or after judgment.
10.5 The Customer shall pay all amounts due under this Agreement in full without any deduction or withholding except as required by law.

**11. DURATION**

11.1 This Agreement shall commence on the Effective Date and shall continue until terminated by either party in accordance with Clause 12 (Termination) (the "Term").
11.2 Each Signed COF shall specify a minimum initial service period (the "Minimum Period"). Upon expiry of the Minimum Period, the relevant Dark Fibre Services shall continue on a rolling [To be confirmed] month-to-month basis until terminated by either party giving [To be confirmed] months' prior written notice.

**12. TERMINATION**

12.1 Either party may terminate this Agreement or any Signed COF by giving written notice to the other if:
    (a) the other party commits a material breach of any term of this Agreement or a Signed COF which is not remedied within [30] days of receipt of a written notice specifying the breach and requiring its remedy;
    (b) the other party becomes insolvent or enters into any form of insolvency proceedings (e.g., administration, liquidation, receivership); or
    (c) a Force Majeure Event continues for a period of more than [90] days.
12.2 The Provider may terminate this Agreement or any Signed COF immediately by written notice if:
    (a) the Customer fails to pay any amount due under this Agreement by the due date on two or more occasions within any 12-month period;
    (b) the Customer breaches Clause 3 (Wayleave) or Clause 7 (Use of Service); or
    (c) there is a change of control of the Customer which, in the Provider's reasonable opinion, adversely affects the Provider's ability to provide the Services or its commercial interests.
12.3 **If the Customer terminates a Signed COF early (i.e., before the expiry of the Minimum Period) for any reason other than the Provider's material breach, the Customer shall pay to the Provider all remaining recurring Charges that would have been payable for the remainder of the Minimum Period.** This is a critical protection for our upfront investment.
12.4 Termination of this Agreement shall automatically terminate all outstanding Signed COFs. Termination of a Signed COF shall not automatically terminate this Agreement.
12.5 Upon termination of this Agreement or a Signed COF for any reason:
    (a) all outstanding Charges shall become immediately due and payable;
    (b) the Customer shall cease to use the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment;
    (c) the Customer shall grant the Provider access to the Site(s) to remove its Equipment; and
    (d) any clauses which by their nature are intended to survive termination (e.g., confidentiality, liability, payment obligations) shall continue in full force and effect.

**13. FORCE MAJEURE**

13.1 Neither party shall be liable for any delay or failure to perform its obligations under this Agreement if such delay or failure is due to a Force Majeure Event.
13.2 A "Force Majeure Event" means any event beyond the reasonable control of the affected party, including acts of God, war, terrorism, riot, civil commotion, fire, flood, earthquake, extreme weather conditions, epidemic, pandemic, industrial disputes, governmental actions, or failure of a utility service or transport network.
13.3 The party affected by a Force Majeure Event shall promptly notify the other party in writing of the nature and extent of the Force Majeure Event and its likely duration.
13.4 The affected party shall use all reasonable endeavours to mitigate the effect of the Force Majeure Event and to resume performance of its obligations as soon as reasonably practicable.

**14. LIMITATIONS OF LIABILITY**

14.1 Nothing in this Agreement shall exclude or limit either party's liability for:
    (a) death or personal injury caused by its negligence;
    (b) fraud or fraudulent misrepresentation; or
    (c) any other liability which cannot be excluded or limited by law.
14.2 Subject to Clause 14.1, the Provider's total aggregate liability to the Customer under or in connection with this Agreement, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), breach of statutory duty, or otherwise, shall in no event exceed:
    (a) in respect of any claim for damage to physical property, [£1,000,000] per event or series of connected events; and
    (b) in respect of all other claims, an amount equal to **[100]% of the total Charges paid or payable by the Customer under this Agreement in the 12 months immediately preceding the event giving rise to the claim.**
    *Quick check: If we set a liability cap >10x annual value, that's a business risk. This 100% of 12-month charges is a common and reasonable cap.*
14.3 **Notwithstanding anything to the contrary in this Agreement, neither party shall be liable to the other for any indirect, special, incidental, punitive, or consequential loss or damage, including, but not limited to, loss of profit, loss of revenue, loss of business, loss of anticipated savings, loss of data, loss of goodwill, or business interruption, howsoever arising, even if advised of the possibility of such damages.** This consequential loss exclusion is **ALWAYS** included and is non-negotiable from our side.
14.4 The Service Credits provided in Schedule 2 shall be the Customer's sole and exclusive remedy for any failure by the Provider to meet the Service Levels.

**15. CONFIDENTIALITY AND IPR**

15.1 Each party undertakes that it shall not at any time during this Agreement, and for a period of [5] years after termination of this Agreement, disclose to any person any confidential information concerning the business, affairs, customers, clients or suppliers of the other party, except as permitted by Clause 15.2.
15.2 Each party may disclose the other party's confidential information:
    (a) to its employees, officers, representatives, subcontractors or advisers who need to know such information for the purposes of carrying out the party's obligations under this Agreement and who are bound by obligations of confidentiality similar to those in this Agreement; and
    (b) as may be required by law, a court of competent jurisdiction or any governmental or regulatory authority.
15.3 Neither party shall use the other party's confidential information for any purpose other than to perform its obligations under this Agreement.
15.4 All intellectual property rights (IPR) in the Dark Fibre Services, Equipment, and any associated documentation or software provided by the Provider shall remain the exclusive property of the Provider or its licensors. The Customer is granted a non-exclusive, non-transferable licence to use such IPR solely for the purpose of receiving and using the Dark Fibre Services.

**16. ENTIRE AGREEMENT AND VARIATIONS**

16.1 This Agreement and any Signed COFs constitute the entire agreement between the parties and supersede all prior discussions, negotiations, and agreements relating to its subject matter.
16.2 Each party acknowledges that in entering into this Agreement it does not rely on, and shall have no remedies in respect of, any statement, representation, assurance or warranty (whether made innocently or negligently) that is not set out in this Agreement.
16.3 No variation of this Agreement or any Signed COF shall be effective unless it is in writing and signed by authorised representatives of both parties.

**17. DATA PROTECTION**

17.1 Both parties shall comply with all applicable data protection laws and regulations, including the UK General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) and the Data Protection Act 2018, in relation to any personal data processed in connection with this Agreement.
17.2 The parties acknowledge that for the purposes of this Agreement, the Provider is generally a processor and the Customer is a controller of any personal data transmitted over the Dark Fibre Services. The Provider's role is limited to providing the physical infrastructure for data transmission and does not involve accessing or processing the content of such data.
17.3 Where the Provider processes any personal data on behalf of the Customer (e.g., contact details for service management), the parties shall enter into a separate Data Processing Addendum if required by law.

**18. GENERAL**

18.1 **Assignment:** Neither party may assign, transfer, charge, or otherwise deal with any of its rights or obligations under this Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party, such consent not to be unreasonably withheld or delayed.
18.2 **Subcontracting:** The Provider may subcontract any of its obligations under this Agreement, but shall remain responsible for the performance of such obligations.
18.3 **No Partnership or Agency:** Nothing in this Agreement is intended to, or shall be deemed to, establish any partnership or joint venture between any of the parties, constitute any party the agent of another party, or authorise any party to make or enter into any commitments for or on behalf of any other party.
18.4 **Severance:** If any provision or part-provision of this Agreement is or becomes invalid, illegal or unenforceable, it shall be deemed modified to the minimum extent necessary to make it valid, legal and enforceable. If such modification is not possible, the relevant provision or part-provision shall be deemed deleted. Any modification to or deletion of a provision or part-provision under this clause shall not affect the validity and enforceability of the rest of this Agreement.
18.5 **Waiver:** A waiver of any right or remedy under this Agreement or by law is only effective if given in writing and shall not be deemed a waiver of any subsequent breach or default. A failure or delay by a party to exercise any right or remedy provided under this Agreement or by law shall not constitute a waiver of that or any other right or remedy, nor shall it prevent or restrict any further exercise of that or any other right or remedy.
18.6 **Third Party Rights:** A person who is not a party to this Agreement shall not have any rights under the Contracts (Rights of Third Parties) Act 1999 to enforce any term of this Agreement.

**19. NOTICES**

19.1 Any notice or other communication given to a party under or in connection with this Agreement shall be in writing and shall be delivered by hand or by pre-paid first-class post or other next working day delivery service at its registered office (or such other address as may have been notified to the other party in accordance with this clause).
19.2 A notice or other communication shall be deemed to have been received:
    (a) if delivered by hand, when left at the address referred to in Clause 19.1; or
    (b) if sent by pre-paid first-class post or other next working day delivery service, at 9.00 am on the second Business Day after posting.
19.3 For the purposes of this Agreement, notice emails shall be sent to:
    **Provider:** [Defaults applied - To be confirmed]
    **Customer:** [Defaults applied - To be confirmed]
    Email notices are for convenience and operational communications only and shall not constitute formal legal notice unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing.

**20. DISPUTE RESOLUTION AND GOVERNING LAW**

20.1 If any dispute arises out of or in connection with this Agreement, the parties shall first attempt to resolve the dispute amicably through good faith negotiations between senior management.
20.2 If the dispute cannot be resolved within [30] days of one party notifying the other of the dispute, either party may refer the dispute to mediation in accordance with the CEDR Model Mediation Procedure.
20.3 If the dispute is not resolved through mediation within [60] days of the mediator's appointment, or if either party refuses to participate in mediation, then the dispute shall be subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales.
20.4 **This Agreement and any dispute or claim arising out of or in connection with it or its subject matter or formation (including non-contractual disputes or claims) shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the law of England and Wales.**

---

**SIGNATURES**

This Agreement has been entered into on the date stated at the beginning of this Agreement.

**FOR AND ON BEHALF OF THE PROVIDER:**

.....................................................
Signature

.....................................................
Name (Print)

.....................................................
Title

**FOR AND ON BEHALF OF THE CUSTOMER:**

.....................................................
Signature

.....................................................
Name (Print)

.....................................................
Title

---

**SCHEDULE 1**

**DEFINITIONS**

In this Agreement, unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms shall have the meanings set out below:

**Agreement:** this Dark Fibre Framework Agreement, including its Schedules and any Signed COFs.
**Business Day:** a day other than a Saturday, Sunday or public holiday in England, when banks in London are open for business.
**Charges:** the fees payable by the Customer to the Provider for the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment, as specified in each Signed COF.
**Confidential Information:** any information, however conveyed or presented, that relates to the business, affairs, operations, customers, processes, product information, know-how, designs, trade secrets or IPR of either party, together with the terms of this Agreement.
**COF (Customer Order Form):** a document, in a form agreed by the parties, used by the Customer to order specific Dark Fibre Services and Equipment under this Agreement.
**Dark Fibre Services:** the provision of unlit, dedicated optical fibre infrastructure and associated Equipment by the Provider to the Customer, as described in a Signed COF.
**Effective Date:** the date specified at the beginning of this Agreement.
**Equipment:** the physical fibre optic cables, associated passive components (e.g., splice enclosures, patch panels), and any other hardware provided by the Provider as part of the Dark Fibre Services, up to the NTP/MMR.
**Force Majeure Event:** has the meaning given in Clause 13.2.
**GBP:** Great British Pounds, the lawful currency of the United Kingdom.
**IRU (Indefeasible Right of Use):** the non-exclusive, non-transferable right granted by the Provider to the Customer to use specified dark fibre capacity for a defined period, without conveying ownership of the underlying physical fibre.
**IPR (Intellectual Property Rights):** patents, utility models, rights to inventions, copyright and related rights, trade marks and service marks, business names and domain names, rights in get-up and trade dress, goodwill and the right to sue for passing off or unfair competition, rights in designs, rights in computer software, database rights, rights to use, and protect the confidentiality of, confidential information (including know-how and trade secrets), and all other intellectual property rights, in each case whether registered or unregistered and including all applications and rights to apply for and be granted, renewals or extensions of, and rights to claim priority from, such rights and all similar or equivalent rights or forms of protection which subsist or will subsist now or in the future in any part of the world.
**Maintenance:** the services provided by the Provider to ensure the ongoing operational integrity and repair of the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment, as described in Clause 6 and Schedule 2.
**Meet-Me-Room (MMR):** a dedicated space within a data centre or exchange where the Provider's fibre physically connects with the Customer's equipment.
**Minimum Period:** the initial minimum service period for a Dark Fibre Service specified in a Signed COF.
**Network Termination Point (NTP):** the physical point at the Customer's premises or designated location where the Provider's Dark Fibre Service terminates and connects to the Customer's network.
**O&M (Operations & Maintenance):** the ongoing charges for the operational management, monitoring, and physical maintenance of the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment.
**Provider:** [Company Name], as identified in the "BETWEEN" clause.
**Ready for Service (RFS):** the point at which the Dark Fibre Services and Equipment have been installed, tested, and are ready for the Customer's use.
**RFS Date:** the date on which the Dark Fibre Services are declared Ready for Service.
**RPI (Retail Price Index):** the index of retail prices published by the Office for National Statistics (or any successor index).
**Service Credits:** the financial credits payable by the Provider to the Customer for failure to meet Service Levels, as set out in Schedule 2.
**Service Levels:** the performance targets and metrics for the Dark Fibre Services, as set out in Schedule 2.
**Signed COF:** a Customer Order Form that has been duly signed by authorised representatives of both the Provider and the Customer.
**Site(s):** the physical location(s) where the Dark Fibre Services are delivered and the Equipment is installed, as specified in a Signed COF.
**Site Wayleave Agreement:** a formal agreement or permission granted by a landowner, landlord, or occupier allowing the Provider to install, operate, and maintain its fibre optic infrastructure on or across their property.
**Term:** the duration of this Agreement, as defined in Clause 11.1.
**TTTR (Target Time to Repair):** the target timeframe within which the Provider aims to restore a physical fibre outage, as detailed in Schedule 2.
**VAT:** Value Added Tax chargeable under the Value Added Tax Act 1994 or any similar sales tax.
**Wayleave:** the right or permission to install and maintain infrastructure (such as fibre optic cables) across land or property belonging to another party.

---

**SCHEDULE 2**

**DARK FIBRE SLAs**

This Schedule sets out the Service Levels for the Dark Fibre Services provided under this Agreement.

**1. SCOPE OF SLAs**
1.1 These Service Levels apply to the physical Dark Fibre Services and Equipment up to the Network Termination Point (NTP) or Meet-Me-Room (MMR) as specified in the Signed COF.
1.2 These Service Levels do not cover Customer-provided equipment, Customer-induced faults, or faults beyond the NTP/MMR.

**2. SERVICE AVAILABILITY**
2.1 **Target Availability:** The Provider aims for 99.9% availability of the Dark Fibre Service per calendar month, excluding planned maintenance.
2.2 **Measurement:** Availability is measured by the total number of minutes in a calendar month minus the total number of minutes of unplanned outage (excluding planned maintenance and Customer-induced faults), divided by the total number of minutes in that month.

**3. FAULT REPORTING AND RESPONSE**
3.1 **Fault Reporting:** The Customer shall report all faults to the Provider's Network Operations Centre (NOC) via [To be confirmed - e.g., dedicated phone number and email] 24 hours a day, 7 days a week.
3.2 **Initial Response Time (IRT):** The Provider shall acknowledge receipt of a fault report and begin diagnostic work within the following target times:
    *   **Priority 1 (P1 - Total Outage):** [15] minutes
    *   **Priority 2 (P2 - Major Degradation):** [30] minutes
    *   **Priority 3 (P3 - Minor Issue):** [60] minutes

**4. TARGET TIME TO REPAIR (TTTR)**
4.1 **TTTR Definition:** The TTTR is the target time from the Provider's acceptance of a fault as a P1 or P2 issue to the restoration of the physical Dark Fibre Service. This covers physical fibre restoration, not software uptime.
4.2 **TTTR Targets:**
    *   **P1 (Total Outage):** [4] hours
    *   **P2 (Major Degradation):** [8] hours
    *   **P3 (Minor Issue):** [Next Business Day]
4.3 **Exclusions from TTTR:** TTTR targets do not apply to:
    *   Faults caused by the Customer or third parties.
    *   Planned maintenance.
    *   Force Majeure Events.
    *   Delays caused by lack of Customer access to sites.
    *   Delays caused by third-party wayleave restrictions outside the Provider's control.

**5. SERVICE CREDIT MECHANISM**
5.1 **Eligibility:** Service Credits are applicable only for P1 (Total Outage) incidents where the TTTR target is missed.
5.2 **Credit Calculation:** For each full hour (or part thereof) that a P1 outage exceeds the TTTR target, the Customer shall receive a Service Credit equal to [0.5]% of the monthly recurring O&M Charge for the affected Dark Fibre Service.
5.3 **Maximum Credit:** The maximum Service Credit for any single P1 outage incident shall not exceed [25]% of the monthly recurring O&M Charge for the affected Dark Fibre Service.
5.4 **Monthly Cap:** The total Service Credits awarded in any calendar month shall not exceed [50]% of the monthly recurring O&M Charge for the affected Dark Fibre Service.
5.5 **Sole Remedy:** Service Credits shall be the Customer’s sole and exclusive remedy for any failure by the Provider to meet the Service Levels.
5.6 **Claim Process:** The Customer must submit a written claim for Service Credits within [30] days of the end of the month in which the outage occurred, providing details of the incident. Service Credits will be applied against the next invoice.

**6. ESCALATION TABLE**

In the event of an unresolved fault or a prolonged outage, the Customer may escalate the issue through the following channels:

| Level | Role/Department           | Response Time Target |
| :---- | :------------------------ | :------------------- |
| 1     | NOC Engineer              | Initial Response     |
| 2     | NOC Team Leader           | Within [1] hour      |
| 3     | Operations Manager        | Within [2] hours     |
| 4     | Head of Operations/CTO    | Within [4] hours     |

---

There you have it. A comprehensive, legal-grade Dark Fibre Framework Agreement. We've covered all the essential clauses, addressed the high-risk areas like Wayleaves and Liability, and integrated our standard commercial terms.

Remember, the clauses marked `[To be confirmed]` will need specific input from the commercial team and the customer. And as always, while this is robust, it's not legal advice – that's for the lawyers. My job is to ensure the commercial and operational risks are managed within the contract's framework.

Let me know if you have any questions or need to discuss any specific points further.
risk:
Perfect! This Dark Fibre Framework Agreement is structured with some excellent commercial protections and clear liability boundaries.
